_meta.description: Mapping of web platform features to recommended polyfill packages and fallback patterns. Used by the web-compat-audit skill to populate remediation recommendations.
_meta.notes: Entries are organized by category. Each entry includes the feature name as used by eslint-plugin-compat and MDN compat data, a recommended polyfill package (if one exists), and a fallback pattern (if no polyfill is suitable or as an alternative).

Array.prototype.at.notes: Widely supported since 2022. Polyfill is trivial.

globalThis.fallback: typeof globalThis !== 'undefined' ? globalThis : typeof window !== 'undefined' ? window : typeof global !== 'undefined' ? global : self

structuredClone.fallback: JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(obj)) for simple objects (loses functions, Date, RegExp, etc.)
structuredClone.notes: Chrome 98+, Firefox 94+, Safari 15.4+. The JSON workaround doesn't handle circular refs or special types.

crypto.randomUUID.fallback: Use uuid package: import { v4 as uuidv4 } from 'uuid'
crypto.randomUUID.notes: Chrome 92+, Firefox 95+, Safari 15.4+. Requires secure context (HTTPS).

AbortController.notes: Not available in IE 11. Polyfill is the recommended approach.

Iterator.prototype.flatMap.notes: Chrome 122+, Firefox 131+, Safari 18.2+. Very recent — iterator helpers are Stage 4 but not yet widely deployed.

Set.prototype.union.notes: Chrome 122+, Firefox 127+, Safari 17+. Set methods are very recent.

IntersectionObserver.fallback: if ('IntersectionObserver' in window) { /* use it */ } else { /* fallback: load all, or use scroll event listener with getBoundingClientRect */ }
IntersectionObserver.notes: Not available in IE 11. Polyfill from W3C is well-maintained.

ResizeObserver.fallback: if ('ResizeObserver' in window) { /* use it */ } else { /* fallback: window resize event */ }

MutationObserver.notes: Widely supported. Only IE 9-10 need the polyfill.

fetch.notes: Not available in IE 11. whatwg-fetch is the standard polyfill.

CustomEvent.notes: Constructor not available in IE 11. The fallback uses the older createEvent API.

requestIdleCallback.fallback: if ('requestIdleCallback' in window) { requestIdleCallback(fn) } else { setTimeout(fn, 1) }
requestIdleCallback.notes: Not available in Safari (any version). setTimeout fallback is the standard approach.

navigator.clipboard.fallback: if (navigator.clipboard) { navigator.clipboard.writeText(text) } else { document.execCommand('copy') with textarea }
navigator.clipboard.notes: Chrome 66+, Firefox 63+, Safari 13.1+. Requires secure context. execCommand is deprecated but works as fallback.

BroadcastChannel.notes: Chrome 54+, Firefox 38+, Safari 15.4+. Not in IE.

FormData (iterator methods).notes: FormData itself is widely supported, but iterator methods (entries, keys, values) are not in IE 11.

TextEncoder.fallback: if ('TextEncoder' in window) { new TextEncoder().encode(str) } else { /* manual UTF-8 encoding */ }
TextEncoder.notes: Chrome 38+, Firefox 19+, Safari 10.1+. Not in IE 11.

TextDecoder.fallback: if ('TextDecoder' in window) { new TextDecoder().decode(buf) } else { /* manual UTF-8 decoding */ }
TextDecoder.notes: Chrome 38+, Firefox 19+, Safari 10.1+. Not in IE 11.

Element.scrollIntoView (smooth).fallback: if ('scrollBehavior' in document.documentElement.style) { el.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth' }) } else { el.scrollIntoView() }
Element.scrollIntoView (smooth).notes: Smooth scrolling: Chrome 61+, Firefox 36+, Safari 15.4+. The basic scrollIntoView() is widely supported.

CSS.supports.fallback: if (window.CSS && window.CSS.supports) { CSS.supports('display', 'grid') } else { /* assume unsupported or use feature detection */ }
CSS.supports.notes: Chrome 28+, Firefox 22+, Safari 9+. Not in IE 11.

PointerEvent.fallback: if (window.PointerEvent) { /* use pointer events */ } else { /* fallback to mouse + touch events */ }
PointerEvent.notes: Chrome 55+, Firefox 59+, Safari 13+. PEP.js is the recommended polyfill.

matchMedia.fallback: if (window.matchMedia) { matchMedia('(max-width: 768px)').matches } else { /* fallback to window.innerWidth check */ }
matchMedia.notes: Chrome 9+, Firefox 6+, Safari 5.1+. Widely supported; only IE 9 needs the polyfill.

Intl.NumberFormat (formatRange).fallback: Manual string concatenation with formatted parts
Intl.NumberFormat (formatRange).notes: Chrome 106+, Firefox 116+, Safari 15.4+. formatRange is relatively new.

Intl.DateTimeFormat (formatRange).fallback: Format start and end dates separately and concatenate

CSS Grid.fallback: Flexbox-based layout as fallback, with @supports (display: grid) { } progressive enhancement
CSS Grid.notes: Supported in all modern browsers. IE 11 has partial support with -ms- prefix and old spec.

CSS Custom Properties.fallback: Hardcoded values with CSS custom properties as progressive enhancement using @supports
CSS Custom Properties.notes: Not available in IE 11. No viable polyfill — use build-time replacement (PostCSS plugin) or hardcoded fallbacks.

CSS Container Queries.fallback: Media queries with assumed container sizes
CSS Container Queries.notes: Chrome 105+, Firefox 110+, Safari 16+. Google's polyfill covers most use cases.

CSS :has() selector.fallback: JavaScript-based class toggling on parent elements
CSS :has() selector.notes: Chrome 105+, Firefox 121+, Safari 15.4+. No CSS-only fallback exists.

CSS Nesting.fallback: Use a CSS preprocessor (Sass/Less) at build time, or flatten selectors manually
CSS Nesting.notes: Chrome 120+, Firefox 117+, Safari 17.2+. Very recent — many target lists will exclude it.

gap (in Flexbox).notes: Flex gap: Chrome 84+, Firefox 63+, Safari 14.1+. Grid gap is more widely supported.

aspect-ratio.fallback: padding-top percentage trick: .container { position: relative; padding-top: 56.25%; } .content { position: absolute; inset: 0; }

backdrop-filter.fallback: Solid semi-transparent background color as fallback
backdrop-filter.notes: Chrome 76+, Firefox 103+, Safari 9+ (-webkit- prefix). Use @supports for progressive enhancement.

scrollbar-color.fallback: @supports not (scrollbar-color: auto) { ::-webkit-scrollbar pseudo-elements for WebKit browsers }
scrollbar-color.notes: Chrome 121+, Firefox 64+, Safari 18.2+ (Newly available Baseline since 2025-12-12). Pair with scrollbar-width: thin.

color-mix().fallback: Pre-computed color values as fallback, with @supports (color: color-mix(in srgb, red, blue)) guard

CSS Subgrid.fallback: Nested grids with explicit track definitions as fallback

view-transition-name.notes: Chrome 111+, Firefox 126+, Safari 18+. Use feature detection; gracefully degrades to instant transitions.

CSS @layer.fallback: Source order specificity management; no CSS-only fallback exists

is-number.notes: Micro-utility; native typeof is universally supported. Always safe to remove.

is-regexp.notes: Micro-utility; instanceof RegExp is universally supported.

is-even.notes: Micro-utility; modulo operator is universally supported.

is-odd.notes: Micro-utility; modulo operator is universally supported.

chalk.notes: picocolors is ~14x smaller. Server-side only, no direct browser compat impact but reduces install size.

whatwg-fetch.notes: Polyfill for fetch API. Check browserslist — if all targets support fetch, this can be removed.

intersection-observer.notes: Polyfill for IntersectionObserver. Check browserslist — if all targets support it natively, this can be removed.

resize-observer-polyfill.notes: Polyfill for ResizeObserver. Baseline Widely available since 2023.

url-polyfill.notes: Polyfill for URL API. Only needed for IE 11.

abortcontroller-polyfill.notes: Polyfill for AbortController. Baseline Widely available.

object-assign.notes: Polyfill for Object.assign. Only needed for IE 11.

array-includes.notes: Polyfill for Array.includes. Only needed for IE 11.

Optional chaining (?.).fallback: Babel plugin: @babel/plugin-transform-optional-chaining. Or manual checks: obj && obj.prop && obj.prop.nested
Optional chaining (?.).notes: Chrome 80+, Firefox 72+, Safari 13.1+. This is a syntax feature — it needs a build-time transform, not a runtime polyfill.

Nullish coalescing (??).fallback: Babel plugin: @babel/plugin-transform-nullish-coalescing-operator. Or manual: value !== null && value !== undefined ? value : fallback
Nullish coalescing (??).notes: Chrome 80+, Firefox 72+, Safari 13.1+. Syntax feature — needs build-time transform.

Private class fields (#).fallback: Babel plugin: @babel/plugin-transform-class-properties. Or WeakMap-based privacy pattern.

Top-level await.fallback: Wrap in async IIFE: (async () => { const data = await fetch(...); })();
Top-level await.notes: Chrome 89+, Firefox 89+, Safari 15+. Only works in ES modules.
